jim HILL HAS
mot GRANTED


0LOFARE

INCREASED RATES ON
COEUR D'ALENE LINE
WILL STAND SMALL
CONCESSION ON SUN
DAY.

NO LOSS OF TRAFFIC

pages.raw_text:
No, the Inland Empire system,
the most recent acquisition of jim
Hill, is not going to restore the old
rates that prevailed prior to the
advent of his control in the prop
erty. The new rates, calling for an
increase of 40 to 60 per cent, took
effect February 1.


A little concession is to be
granted, such as reduced Sunday
rate to Liberty Lake and st. Joe
and ST. Maries, but that will spell
the extent of the hoped for reduc-
ton, due to the protest over the
rate increase.


There will be no reduction in
the passenger tariff that went into
effect February L." was announced
yesterday at the general passenger
agents office at the Terminal build
Ing. However. we are preparing a
schedule of Sunday rates that will
grant lower fares to Liberty Lake
and st. Joe and st. Maries."


"Will you give Sunday rate to
Occur d'Alene?" was asked.


"There will probably be none
granted to Occur d'Alene. However,
that is a matter that we connot def.
initely announce at this time, as the
summer schedule of rates for Sun
day have not yet been announced"
was the reply.


"How about the slump in your
traffic to Occur d'Alene this week
because Of the Increase in fare Has
1t hit you as hard as people make
out?" was asked.


Traffic seems to be going ahead
In the accustomed way. If there has
been a falling off in the Occur
d'Alene traffic, we have not felt It"
came the answer.
